---
title: "Quickstart"
description: "Deploy an agent in ~10 minutes — interactive or fully unattended."
---

<Info>
Lowkey needs credentials for an AWS account you own (a dedicated sandbox is recommended — the builder profile gets AdministratorAccess). Bedrock-native agents require Bedrock access in the chosen region.
</Info>

## Prerequisites

<Steps>
  <Step title="AWS credentials configured">
    ```bash
    aws sts get-caller-identity
    ```
    Must return your account ID. Use a dedicated sandbox account; never deploy into production.
  </Step>
  <Step title="A supported shell">
    Works in `bash`, `zsh`, and AWS CloudShell.
  </Step>
  <Step title="(Optional) Bedrock access in your region">
    Required for OpenClaw, Claude Code, NemoClaw, Hermes, Pi, IronClaw. Not needed for Codex CLI (OpenAI) or Kiro CLI (Kiro cloud).
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Interactive install

```bash
curl -sfL install.lowkey.run | bash
```

You'll be prompted for:

1. **Install mode** — Simple (recommended) or Advanced
2. **Agent pack** — openclaw, claude-code, codex-cli, kiro-cli, nemoclaw, hermes, pi, ironclaw
3. **Profile** — builder, account_assistant, or personal_assistant
4. **Deploy method** — CloudFormation or Terraform

Simple mode auto-configures everything else from the pack + profile choice. See [Simple-mode defaults](/reference/simple-mode-defaults) for what's decided for you.

## Unattended install

Pass `-y` (or `--non-interactive`) plus the pack and profile to skip prompts entirely.

<Tabs>
  <Tab title="OpenClaw (recommended)">
    ```bash
    curl -sfL install.lowkey.run | bash -s -- -y \
      --pack openclaw \
      --profile builder
    ```
  </Tab>
  <Tab title="Claude Code">
    ```bash
    curl -sfL install.lowkey.run | bash -s -- -y \
      --pack claude-code \
      --profile builder
    ```
  </Tab>
  <Tab title="Codex CLI">
    ```bash
    curl -sfL install.lowkey.run | bash -s -- -y \
      --pack codex-cli \
      --profile builder
    ```
    Then SSM into the instance and run `codex login` — Codex auth is deferred until after deploy.
  </Tab>
  <Tab title="Kiro CLI (headless)">
    ```bash
    # First store your Kiro API key in Secrets Manager
    aws secretsmanager create-secret \
      --name /lowkey/kiro-api-key \
      --secret-string "$KIRO_API_KEY"

    curl -sfL install.lowkey.run | bash -s -- -y \
      --pack kiro-cli \
      --profile builder \
      --kiro-from-secret /lowkey/kiro-api-key
    ```
  </Tab>
</Tabs>

## What happens next

<AccordionGroup>
  <Accordion title="CloudFormation / Terraform creates the base infrastructure">
    New VPC (`10.0.0.0/16` by default), public subnet, security group, IAM instance profile, and an EC2 instance (`t4g.xlarge` for builder, `t4g.medium` for assistant profiles).
  </Accordion>
  <Accordion title="UserData bootstrap runs on the instance">
    Installs Node.js via mise, clones the lowkey repo, dispatches to the pack's `install.sh`, and signals CFN / Terraform when done.
  </Accordion>
  <Accordion title="Pack installs the agent">
    Each pack has its own logic — systemd service, CLI binary, or both. See the per-agent pages under [Agents](/agents/overview) for specifics.
  </Accordion>
  <Accordion title="Security services turn on (builder / account_assistant)">
    Security Hub, GuardDuty, Inspector, Access Analyzer, Config recorder. Personal assistant profile keeps them off.
  </Accordion>
  <Accordion title="Connect via SSM">
    ```bash
    aws ssm start-session --target i-XXXXXXXXX
    ```
    SSH is disabled by default (`SSHAllowedCidr=127.0.0.1/32`). Use SSM Session Manager.
  </Accordion>
</AccordionGroup>

## Verify

Once the stack is `CREATE_COMPLETE`, SSM in and run the agent's command — e.g. `openclaw --version`, `claude --version`, `codex --version`, `kiro-cli --version`. The pack's post-install notice tells you what to run next (typically auth or first-run config).

## Clean up

```bash
# CloudFormation
aws cloudformation delete-stack --stack-name <your-stack>

# Terraform
terraform destroy
```

This removes everything the installer created: VPC, EC2, IAM, security-service subscriptions. Any Bedrock model invocations are billed through your account and stop when the instance terminates.
